perturbation.original_text: 3. Nondisclosure. The Receiving Party agrees that, in consideration of access to the Confidential Information, it shall hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall take all measures necessary to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of the persons not bound to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Nondisclosure. The Receiving Party agrees that, in consideration of access to the Confidential Materials, it shall hold such Confidential Materials in strict confidence and shall take all measures necessary to prevent the Confidential Materials from falling into the public domain or into the possession of the persons not bound to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The document uses 'Confidential Information' and later switches to 'Confidential Materials' without clarifying whether the terms are interchangeable, or have differing definitions. This creates an ambiguity regarding the scope of the nondisclosure obligations, affecting the Receiving Party’s obligations throughout the document.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Definition. “Confidential Information” shall, mean all intellectual property, business plans, financial records and strategies, marketing plans, contacts, trade secrets, information, materials, documentation and software, as well as copies of all such materials made thereof as authorized by this Agreement, including written, oral, or verbal disclosures. “Confidential Information” shall not include: a. Information that was in the public domain, in its entirety in a unified form, at the time of disclosure to Receiving Party by Vapotherm; b. Information known to Receiving Party prior to its disclosure by Vapotherm, such prior knowledge to be demonstrated by Receiving Party’s records prepared before the date the Confidential Information is disclosed by Vapotherm; c. Information that becomes part of the public domain after the date of disclosure by Vapotherm through no fault of Receiving Party; d. Information that is disclosed by a third party to Receiving Party after the date of disclosure by Vapotherm, where the third party did not require Receiving Party to hold such information in confidence and did not acquire such information directly or indirectly from Vapotherm.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'Confidential Information', which is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text' section. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Materials' without defining this new term. This creates ambiguity because it's unclear whether 'Confidential Materials' has the same scope as the defined 'Confidential Information'. If 'Confidential Materials' has a different scope, it directly contradicts the obligations outlined in the contradicted section, which refers specifically to 'Confidential Information'. This is a strong contradiction according to the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4. Receiving Party’s Procedures. Receiving Party shall disclose Confidential Information only to those of its employees, agents and independent contractors who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.changed_text: 4. Receiving Party’s Procedures. Receiving Party shall disclose Protected Information only to those of its employees, agents and independent contractors who have a need to know such information for the Permitted Purpose.
perturbation.explanation: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' introduces ambiguity, particularly when other sections refer to 'Confidential Information', without defining 'Protected Information.' This leads to uncertainty regarding to which information the procedure applies to, specially the need to know, creating a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Nondisclosure. The Receiving Party agrees that, in consideration of access to the Confidential Information, it shall hold such Confidential Information in strict confidence and shall take all measures necessary to prevent the Confidential Information from falling into the public domain or into the possession of the persons not bound to this Agreement. The Receiving Party further agrees: a. To use Confidential Information for the Permitted Purpose; b. Not to disclose Confidential Information to any other entity, except as expressly authorized in writing by Vapotherm; c. Not to use Confidential Information in such a way as to hinder, interfere with, or in any way circumvent Vapotherm’s interest in the Technology, or consulting or business relations between and among Vapotherm and third parties; d. Not to copy Confidential Information in whole or in part, without the express written permission of Vapotherm; e. To return Confidential Information, including all copies and records thereof, to Vapotherm, at the Receiving Party’s expense, within five (5) business days after: (i) receipt of a written request from Vapotherm; (ii) a decision by Receiving Party not to enter into any consulting relationship with Vapotherm; or, (iii) within thirty (30) days following the termination of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Protected Information' in Section 4 creates a contradiction with Section 3, which refers exclusively to 'Confidential Information'. The change introduces a new term, 'Protected Information', which is not defined in the contract. Since Section 3 mandates specific actions regarding 'Confidential Information', while Section 4 now discusses 'Protected Information', it becomes unclear whether the obligations in Section 3 apply to the 'Protected Information'. This misalignment of terminology, according to the perturbation definition, leads to uncertainty and therefore creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Injunctive Relief. Because of the unique nature of the Confidential information, the Receiving Party understands and agrees that Vapotherm will suffer irreparable harm in the event that any party hereto fails to comply with any of the terms of the Agreement, and monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate such breach.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Injunctive Relief. Because of the unique nature of the Proprietary Data, the Receiving Party understands and agrees that Vapotherm will suffer irreparable harm in the event that any party hereto fails to comply with any of the terms of the Agreement, and monetary damages may be inadequate to compensate such breach.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement mentions 'Confidential Information', defining its scope. By changing this to 'Proprietary Data' in the context of injunctive relief, it creates uncertainty whether a breach related to information defined under 'Confidential Information' will be subject to the same injunctive remedy, since 'Proprietary Data' isn't defined, hence it creates a contradiction between the scope and the remedy available.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Definition. “Confidential Information” shall, mean all intellectual property, business plans, financial records and strategies, marketing plans, contacts, trade secrets, information, materials, documentation and software, as well as copies of all such materials made thereof as authorized by this Agreement, including written, oral, or verbal disclosures. “Confidential Information” shall not include: a. Information that was in the public domain, in its entirety in a unified form, at the time of disclosure to Receiving Party by Vapotherm; b. Information known to Receiving Party prior to its disclosure by Vapotherm, such prior knowledge to be demonstrated by Receiving Party’s records prepared before the date the Confidential Information is disclosed by Vapotherm; c. Information that becomes part of the public domain after the date of disclosure by Vapotherm through no fault of Receiving Party; d. Information that is disclosed by a third party to Receiving Party after the date of disclosure by Vapotherm, where the third party did not require Receiving Party to hold such information in confidence and did not acquire such information directly or indirectly from Vapotherm.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information,' which is a defined term in the contract. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Data,' which is undefined. The contradicted text explicitly defines 'Confidential Information,' outlining its scope and exclusions. By using 'Proprietary Data' in the changed text, the agreement becomes ambiguous as to whether the injunctive relief applies to the defined scope of 'Confidential Information.' This creates an in-text contradiction because the remedy (injunctive relief) is now potentially decoupled from the clearly defined 'Confidential Information,' which is a misaligned terminology leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Inventions. Inventions means all discoveries, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others: (i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Discoveries. Discoveries means all inventions, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others: (i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Inventions' is replaced with 'Discoveries' in the definition of Section 7, this creates contradiction in the text. The main agreement refers to 'Inventions' being exclusive property of Vapotherm. By using the term 'Discoveries' now, it's is uncertain if the term refers to the same subject, and therefore the same property rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Inventions. Inventions means all discoveries, improvements, modifications and enhancements relating to the Technology that are conceived, developed or reduced to practice by Receiving Party during die Term, either solely or jointly with others: (i) during the course of or as a result of performing the Permitted Purpose, or (ii) that are -2- related to or attributable to Vapotherm’s products, or later modifications thereof, whether patentable or not, and all Intellectual Property rights therein. All Inventions will be immediately reported to Vapotherm and shall be the exclusive property of Vapotherm. The Receiving Party will perform, at Vapotherm’s request and expense, any act which it can reasonably perform that Vapotherm deems necessary to vest title to such inventions and ideas in Vapotherm and to execute any and all patent applications in connection therewith.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Inventions' with 'Discoveries' in Section 7's definition. The contradicted text later refers to 'Inventions' as the exclusive property of Vapotherm. By changing the defined term from 'Inventions' to 'Discoveries' in the first instance, the agreement now contains a contradiction because the 'Inventions' referred to later in the document are no longer clearly defined. This creates uncertainty about which discoveries belong exclusively to Vapotherm, and which do not.

perturbation.original_text: 10. No Warranties. The Receiving Party acknowledges that Confidential Information may still be under development, or may be incomplete, and that such information may relate to products that are under development or are planned for development.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. No Warranties. The Receiving Party acknowledges that Technical Data may still be under development, or may be incomplete, and that such information may relate to products that are under development or are planned for development.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Technical Data' alters the context of the warranty disclaimer. It creates an ambiguity whether the exclusion of warranties applies specifically to the 'Confidential Information' or broadly to 'Technical Data,' which lacks a definition. This contradiction affects the liability of Vapotherm and creates uncertainty on which data/information there are no warraties for.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1. Definition. “Confidential Information” shall, mean all intellectual property, business plans, financial records and strategies, marketing plans, contacts, trade secrets, information, materials, documentation and software, as well as copies of all such materials made thereof as authorized by this Agreement, including written, oral, or verbal disclosures. “Confidential Information” shall not include: a. Information that was in the public domain, in its entirety in a unified form, at the time of disclosure to Receiving Party by Vapotherm; b. Information known to Receiving Party prior to its disclosure by Vapotherm, such prior knowledge to be demonstrated by Receiving Party’s records prepared before the date the Confidential Information is disclosed by Vapotherm; c. Information that becomes part of the public domain after the date of disclosure by Vapotherm through no fault of Receiving Party; d. Information that is disclosed by a third party to Receiving Party after the date of disclosure by Vapotherm, where the third party did not require Receiving Party to hold such information in confidence and did not acquire such information directly or indirectly from Vapotherm.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is a defined term in the contract as outlined in the contradicted text. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Technical Data', which is not defined in the contract. This misaligned terminology creates a contradiction because the contract now disclaims warranties for 'Technical Data' without specifying what that encompasses. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty in what the warranty disclaimer applies to, and therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
